description: Quiz testing comprehension of 'The Swan King' Chapter 9 from Class 6 Roots and Wings literature reader, including plot, characters, and language skills.

questions.question: Who was the golden swan king that led the flock in 'The Swan King'?
questions.explanation: Sakula was the golden swan king who led the entire flock with wisdom and nobility.

questions.question: Why did the swans want to travel to the Lotus Lake?
options: Because they heard it was a beautiful place with abundant grass
To learn wisdom from a human king
questions.correctAnswer: Because they heard it was a beautiful place with abundant grass
questions.explanation: The swans were attracted to the Lotus Lake in Chitrakoot because they had heard it was a beautiful and peaceful place with plenty of food.

questions.question: Which swan refused to leave his trapped companion and stayed behind?
questions.explanation: Sumukha was the chief of the swan army who remained loyal and refused to abandon Dhritarashtra when he was caught in the net.

questions.question: According to the story, what is the most powerful way to win over another person?
questions.explanation: The central lesson of 'The Swan King' teaches that a person can be won over through love and respect rather than through punishment or fear.

questions.question: True or False: Dhritarashtra was the swan king who led the entire flock.
questions.explanation: Sakula was the swan king who led the flock. Dhritarashtra was a swan who got caught in the hunter's trap.

questions.question: True or False: The hunter stayed silent while the swans were feeding so they would not get frightened and fly away hungry.
questions.explanation: The hunter remained silent during the swans' meal time to prevent them from panicking and flying away before eating, ensuring they would be weak and unable to fly far.
